title: HK Dog Vaccination Requirements - Research Findings

resident_dogs.description: Requirements for dogs residing in Hong Kong

rabies.requirement: All dogs aged over 5 months must be vaccinated against rabies

microchipping.requirement: All dogs must be microchipped for identification
microchipping.timing: Before or at the same time as rabies vaccination

licensing.requirement: All dogs aged 5 months or above must be licensed
licensing.renewal: Must be renewed every 3 years with rabies revaccination

core_vaccines.name: DHPPi (or DHPPiL)
core_vaccines.frequency: First series: 3 vaccines at 8, 12, 16 weeks; then annual booster

core_vaccines.name: Leptospirosis
core_vaccines.covers: Bacterial infection spread through contaminated water/soil
core_vaccines.frequency: Annual booster (often combined with DHPPi as DHPPiL)
core_vaccines.note: Important in HK due to urban/rural interface

non_core_vaccines.name: Kennel Cough (Bordetella)
non_core_vaccines.recommendation: For dogs frequently exposed to other pets (kennels, dog parks, grooming)
non_core_vaccines.frequency: Annual or every 6 months for high-risk

imported_dogs.description: Requirements for dogs being imported into Hong Kong

requirements: Health certificate issued within 14 days of travel

Group_II.quarantine: Minimum 4 months at owner's expense (or return to origin)
requirements: Rabies vaccination 30 days to 12 months before arrival

Group_IIIA.description: Higher rabies risk countries/places (excluding Mainland China)
requirements: Rabies vaccination 30 days to 12 months before arrival
Rabies vaccination 30 days to 12 months before arrival

Mainland_China.requirements: All relevant import requirements must be met

vaccination_timing_puppies.rabies: Can be given at 5 months (required for license)
vaccination_timing_puppies.note: Puppies should complete initial series before exposure to unfamiliar dogs

pre_vaccination: Check for history of allergic reactions to vaccinations
Ensure dog is healthy (no vomiting/diarrhea in past few days)
post_vaccination_monitoring: Watch for allergic reactions within first few hours
Symptoms: vomiting, breathing difficulty, rash, facial swelling, injection site swelling
Seek veterinary treatment if symptoms occur

notes: Rabies vaccination is legally required, not just recommended
License and vaccination go hand-in-hand (3-year cycle)
Import requirements vary significantly by country of origin
